hero.headline: Paid family and sick leave — Massachusetts
hero.subhead: 0.88% PFML plus 40-hour sick leave. Here's what you owe.
hero.partner_badge: Served by Teamed partner network: Suna Solutions, Inc.

answer_capsule: Massachusetts employers with 25 or more covered individuals pay a combined 0.88% PFML contribution on wages up to the Social Security wage base ($176,100 in 2026) under M.G.L. c. 175M. Benefits reach up to 20 weeks for medical leave and 12 weeks for family leave, capped at $1,230.39 per week. Separately, M.G.L. c. 149 § 148C mandates 1 hour of paid sick time per 30 hours worked (40-hour annual cap) for employers with 11 or more employees. Teamed handles contribution withholding, remittance, and leave coordination through its US partner network.

pfml_contribution_block.question: What are the Massachusetts PFML contribution rates in 2026?
pfml_contribution_block.answer_first: Massachusetts employers with 25 or more employees pay a minimum 0.42% employer share. The combined rate is 0.88% of covered wages — split 0.70% for medical leave and 0.18% for family leave.
pfml_contribution_block.body:
The Massachusetts Paid Family and Medical Leave (PFML) contribution is 0.88% of covered wages for employers with 25 or more covered individuals, capped at the Social Security wage base of $176,100 in 2026. You split the 0.88% this way: the 0.70% medical leave portion divides at a minimum 0.42% employer / 0.28% employee maximum; the 0.18% family leave portion is entirely employee-borne — the employer owes nothing on this slice.

Employers with fewer than 25 covered individuals pay no employer share at all. The full 0.46% deducted from the employee's wages covers their obligation.

Rates are unchanged from 2025, confirmed by the MA Department of Family and Medical Leave (DFML) October 2025 notice. Contributions remit quarterly to the DFML. Teamed's partner handles the payroll calculation, employee deduction, and quarterly remittance on your behalf — it appears as a separate itemised line on every invoice.

pfml_benefits_block.question: How much does Massachusetts PFML pay, and for how long?
pfml_benefits_block.answer_first: Massachusetts PFML pays 64% of the employee's average weekly wage, up to a weekly cap of $1,230.39 in 2026. Medical leave runs up to 20 weeks; family leave runs up to 12 weeks; the combined annual maximum is 26 weeks.
pfml_benefits_block.body:
The $1,230.39 weekly cap applies from January 2026 (up from $1,170.64 in 2025), adjusted annually based on the state average weekly wage. For an employee earning $80,000 per year — about $1,538 per week — the replacement rate at 64% is roughly $985 per week, well below the cap. Higher earners hit the cap quickly: anyone on a weekly wage above $1,922 will receive exactly $1,230.39 per week regardless of salary.

The job-protection provision is the operational concern for employers. Massachusetts PFML provides reinstatement rights after covered leave. You must restore the employee to the same or equivalent position. Terminating an employee while on PFML leave, or constructively forcing them out, is a protected-leave violation.

The employee must give 30 days advance notice where foreseeable. For unforeseeable leave (serious illness, emergency), they notify as soon as practicable.

leave_table.type: Family leave (bond with new child, care for family member, military qualifying)

sick_time_block.question: How does Massachusetts earned sick time work for employers?
sick_time_block.answer_first: Employees accrue 1 hour of sick time per 30 hours worked, up to 40 hours per year. Employers with 11 or more employees must provide paid sick time; those with fewer than 11 provide unpaid sick time.
sick_time_block.body:
The Massachusetts Earned Sick Time Law under M.G.L. c. 149 § 148C is separate from PFML. It covers all private-sector employees, including part-time and temporary workers. Accrual begins on the first day of employment, but the employee cannot use accrued time until 90 days have passed.

For a full-time employee working 40 hours per week, accrual runs at roughly 1.33 hours per week — the 40-hour annual cap hits in about 30 weeks. Accrued but unused sick time carries over year to year, subject to the 40-hour carryover cap. You can cap use at 40 hours per calendar year even if the employee has carried over time.

A common mistake: employers confuse the 'employee headcount for paid vs unpaid threshold' with a total headcount. The threshold is 11 or more 'employees' — count every employee, including part-time and seasonal, not just full-time equivalents. If you're at 10 employees and hire one part-time worker, you cross the paid threshold immediately.

pfml_vs_fmla_block.question: What is the difference between Massachusetts PFML and federal FMLA?
pfml_vs_fmla_block.answer_first: Massachusetts PFML provides paid leave funded by contributions; federal FMLA provides unpaid, job-protected leave for employers with 50 or more employees. The two can run concurrently. PFML covers smaller employers — down to one employee.
pfml_vs_fmla_block.body:
Federal FMLA only covers employers with 50 or more employees within 75 miles. Massachusetts PFML covers nearly all employers and employees, regardless of company size — there is no minimum employer headcount for PFML to apply. That means a Massachusetts startup with 3 employees owes PFML obligations even though federal FMLA does not apply.

Where an employee qualifies for both (employer has 50+ employees, employee meets FMLA service requirements), the leaves run concurrently. The employer cannot require the employee to exhaust PFML first and then take FMLA separately. Coordination is the employer's administrative responsibility.

Key PFML-only benefits not in FMLA: paid replacement income (PFML pays; FMLA does not), military family qualifying exigency leave under PFML, and leave to care for a covered family member with a serious health condition (PFML includes domestic partners; FMLA does not).

good_to_know.question: What should employers know before hiring in Massachusetts?
good_to_know.body: Massachusetts has one of the strictest worker classification tests in the United States. The ABC test under M.G.L. c. 149 § 148B presumes every worker is an employee unless the employer proves all three prongs: independence from control, work outside the employer's usual course of business, and independent business establishment. Prong B is the sticking point for tech and professional services — a software developer doing the employer's core work fails it immediately. Misclassification triggers treble damages and civil and criminal liability. Patriots Day (third Monday in April) is a Massachusetts-only public holiday; build it into employment contracts and payroll calendars. Final pay on termination is due immediately — not on the next scheduled payday.

pullquote.speaker: Teamed Legal Operations
pullquote.text: Massachusetts PFML surprises most out-of-state employers on two fronts: the coverage threshold is zero — one employee is enough to trigger obligations — and the 30-day advance notice rule creates an administrative gap if you haven't built it into your onboarding process. Get the remittance cadence right from day one; the DFML does not send warnings.
pullquote.provenance_note: No MI match found; attributed to Teamed Legal Operations per pullquote provenance rule.

faq.q: What is the Massachusetts PFML contribution rate in 2026?
faq.a: The combined rate is 0.88% of covered wages (up to the Social Security wage base) for employers with 25 or more covered individuals. Employers with fewer than 25 covered individuals pay no employer share — the employee pays 0.46%. Rates are unchanged from 2025 per MA DFML October 2025 notice.

faq.q: How many weeks of paid family leave does Massachusetts provide?
faq.a: Massachusetts PFML provides up to 12 weeks for family leave (bonding, caring for a family member, military qualifying exigency) and up to 20 weeks for the employee's own serious health condition. The combined annual maximum is 26 weeks. Weekly benefits are capped at $1,230.39 in 2026.

faq.q: Does Massachusetts require employers to provide paid sick leave?
faq.a: Yes, if you have 11 or more employees. Under M.G.L. c. 149 § 148C, employees accrue 1 hour of paid sick time per 30 hours worked, up to 40 hours per year. Employers with fewer than 11 employees must still provide sick time, but unpaid.

faq.q: What is the difference between Massachusetts PFML and federal FMLA?
faq.a: PFML is paid leave funded by employer and employee contributions and covers employers of any size. FMLA is unpaid and only covers employers with 50 or more employees within 75 miles. Where both apply, leaves run concurrently.

meta.title: Massachusetts PFML and Paid Sick Leave 2026 | Teamed
meta.description: Massachusetts employers: 0.88% PFML on wages up to $176,100, 20-week medical leave, $1,230.39/wk cap, plus 40-hr earned sick time under M.G.L. c. 149 § 148C.
